original_abstract:
This comprehensive guide explores the critical role of data governance in the modern digital era, where data is frequently characterized as an organization's most valuable asset. Moving beyond the technical basics, the text provides a holistic roadmap for professionals to manage the availability, integrity, and security of their data ecosystems. It establishes a strong foundational understanding of core principles like data quality, transparency, and lifecycle management while detailing the structural frameworks—such as DAMA-DMBOK and CMMI—necessary for organizational success.

The guide delves into the practical execution of governance programs, addressing the development of policies and standards, the selection of supporting technologies, and the formation of effective, cross-functional teams involving data stewards and owners. Recognizing that technology alone is insufficient, the text emphasizes the human element, offering strategies for change management, leadership, and fostering a data-centric culture to overcome institutional resistance.

Furthermore, the work examines the intersection of governance with global regulatory landscapes (such as GDPR and HIPAA) and security imperatives to protect sensitive information and build customer trust. Through industry-specific case studies and the application of maturity models, the guide illustrates how robust governance serves as a competitive advantage. Finally, it anticipates the future of the field, preparing readers for the transformative impacts of artificial intelligence, blockchain, and evolving data ethics.
humanized_abstract:
Data Governance: An Enterprise Initiative Data Governance, in the age of digital data, where data is typically deemed to be the most valuable resource in any organization, is more than just a technological function. Data Governance: An Enterprise Initiative presents a comprehensive and holistic approach to data governance, data management, and related services. Moving beyond the fundamental aspects of data governance, this book aims to provide a complete guide that not only ensures that data is available, usable, reliable, and secure but also gives data governance practitioners the knowledge and skills required to successfully plan, execute, and sustain an enterprise data governance initiative. With a solid grounding in data quality, data transparency, and data lifecycle management, this book also covers business models, data governance frameworks, standards, architecture, and governance models including DAMA-DMBOK and CMMI.

The second volume of the Data Governance Handbook Series goes into the details of how to implement and run a Data Governance Program. Topics include: - Data governance policy and standards development - Technology selection and implementation - The role and structure of data steward and data owner teams The first section discusses the technology needed to support governance as well as the people work required to facilitate change and build leadership support to help combat the cultural battles that often arise when trying to implement data governance practices.

It delves into the interplay of governance with international regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA, as well as the importance of data protection and customer satisfaction to ensure the security of an organization’s data. In addition, this work through examples and maturity models carried out in each industry illustrates how good governance can become a competitive element. It finally looks ahead to the future of the field, preparing the reader for the disruptions brought by technologies such as artificial intelligence, blockchain and new data ethics.
